title: MCP Server Tool Integration
class_hierarchy: MCPServer (abstract ABC) — defines connect(), cleanup(), list_tools(), call_tool()
_MCPServerWithClientSession(MCPServer, ABC) — adds ClientSession, caching, filtering, retries
interaction_flow: User/App → MCPServerManager: async with MCPServerManager(servers) as manager
_connect_all_parallel: asyncio.gather(_attempt_connect per server, raise_on_error=False) → check strict
_ServerWorker._run loop: dequeues _ServerCommand → calls server.connect() or server.cleanup() with timeout
_MCPServerWithClientSession.connect(): create_streams() via exit_stack → transport (read, write, rest)
connect: ClientSession(read, write, timeout) via exit_stack
connect: await session.initialize() → stores server_initialize_result + self.session
list_tools: check cache → if cache_tools_list and not _cache_dirty and _tools_list: return cached
_run_with_retries: retry loop up to max_retry_attempts with exponential backoff
list_tools: if tool_filter → _apply_tool_filter(tools, run_context, agent) → filtered_tools
_apply_tool_filter: static dict → _apply_static_tool_filter (allowed_tool_names allowlist then blocked_tool_names blocklist)
_apply_dynamic_tool_filter: builds ToolFilterContext(run_context, agent, server_name); for each tool calls tool_filter_func(context, tool); awaits if coroutine
call_tool: _validate_required_parameters(tool_name, arguments) — checks required fields from cached schema
call_tool: if meta is None → _run_with_retries(session.call_tool(tool_name, arguments))
call_tool: if meta present → _run_with_retries(session.call_tool(tool_name, arguments, meta=meta))
Note: tool_meta_resolver is invoked by the Agents SDK (MCPUtil layer) BEFORE calling call_tool to inject _meta
Policy forms: bool, dict[tool_name→bool], callable(run_context, agent, tool)→bool
MCPUtil wraps each MCPTool as FunctionTool with needs_approval from _get_needs_approval_for_tool
Runner checks needs_approval before invoking call_tool; may pause for human approval
key_relationships: MCPServerManager creates _ServerWorker per server (parallel mode only)
_ServerWorker wraps MCPServer with asyncio.Queue-based command dispatch
list_tools() calls _apply_tool_filter() which uses ToolFilterContext with run_context + agent
call_tool() uses session.call_tool() with optional _meta dict from tool_meta_resolver

tool_filter_types.Callable: dynamic filter: fn(ToolFilterContext, MCPTool) -> bool | Awaitable[bool]

transport_differences.MCPServerStreamableHttp: create_streams() uses streamablehttp_client; supports session isolation, _serialize_session_requests, GetSessionIdCallback

important_notes: cache_tools_list=True avoids per-run list_tools() round-trips; invalidate via invalidate_tools_cache()
connect_in_parallel=True uses _ServerWorker tasks to maintain task-affinity for AnyIO cancel scopes
drop_failed_servers=True (default): active_servers excludes failed ones; strict=True raises on first failure
tool_meta_resolver injects per-call _meta (tenant IDs, trace context) before session.call_tool()
failure_error_function controls whether MCP tool errors become model-visible text or raised exceptions
MCPServerStreamableHttp supports shared vs isolated sessions (_serialize_session_requests)
